CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_onboarding_tasks_by_department()
 RETURNS jsonb
 LANGUAGE sql
 STABLE
AS $function$
  SELECT jsonb_agg(
           jsonb_build_object(
             'department', name,
             'tasks', tasks_json
           )
         )
  FROM (
    SELECT 
      name,
      jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
          'id', id,
          'description', description,
          'status', status
        ) ORDER BY id
      ) AS tasks_json
    FROM tasks
    WHERE task_type = 'on_boarding'
    GROUP BY name
    ORDER BY name
  ) AS grouped;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_punctuality_report(p_year integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_month integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_name_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, total_on_days text, total_lates bigint, score_out_of_5 numeric, from_month_year text, to_month_year text, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE
AS $function$
DECLARE
  _year_int       INTEGER;
  _month_int      INTEGER;
  _start_date     DATE;
  _end_date       DATE;
  _total_weekdays INTEGER;
BEGIN
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 1) Default to current year/month if no arguments provided
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  IF p_year IS NULL THEN
    _year_int := EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT;
  ELSE
    _year_int := p_year;
  END IF;

  IF p_month IS NULL THEN
    _month_int := EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT;
  ELSE
    _month_int := p_month;
  END IF;

  -- 2) Compute first‐day and last‐day of that month
  _start_date := MAKE_DATE(_year_int, _month_int, 1);
  _end_date   := (_start_date + INTERVAL '1 MONTH')::DATE - 1;  
  -- e.g. if month=12, this yields 2024-12-31

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 3) Count how many “workdays” (Mon→Fri) exist in that month
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  SELECT COUNT(*)
    INTO _total_weekdays
    FROM GENERATE_SERIES(_start_date, _end_date, '1 day') AS g(day_dt)
   WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM g.day_dt) < 6;  -- ISODOW: 1=Mon .. 5=Fri

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 4) Build the filtered employee set, then join logs, then aggregate
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  RETURN QUERY
    WITH filtered_emps AS (
      SELECT
        e.id                        AS employee_id,
        e.employee_code             AS employee_code,
        c.first_name,
        c.second_name,
        c.third_name,
        c.forth_name,
        CONCAT_WS(
          ' ',
          c.first_name,
          c.second_name,
          c.third_name,
          c.forth_name
        )                            AS full_name,
        e.shift_start_time          AS shift_start_time,
        e.shift_end_time            AS shift_end_time,
        et.rules                     AS employment_rule
      FROM public.employees e
      JOIN public.candidates c
        ON c.id = e.candidate_id
      LEFT JOIN public.employment_types et
        ON et.id = e.employment_type_id
      WHERE
        -- (a) If p_employee_id is supplied, only that employee; otherwise all
        ( p_employee_id IS NULL OR e.id = p_employee_id )
        AND
        -- (b) If name search is supplied, filter by concatenated full name
        ( p_name_search IS NULL
          OR CONCAT_WS(
               ' ',
               c.first_name,
               c.second_name,
               c.third_name,
               c.forth_name
             ) ILIKE '%' || p_name_search || '%' )
    ),

    logs_in_month AS (
      SELECT
        dl.employee_id,
        dl.date           AS log_date,
        dl.check_in_time,
        dl.check_out_time
      FROM public.daily_attendance_logs dl
      WHERE dl.date BETWEEN _start_date AND _end_date
        AND EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM dl.date) < 6  -- only Mon→Fri
    ),

    emp_logs AS (
      SELECT
        fe.employee_id,
        fe.employee_code,
        fe.full_name,
        fe.shift_start_time,
        fe.shift_end_time,
        fe.employment_rule,
        lm.log_date,
        lm.check_in_time,
        lm.check_out_time
      FROM filtered_emps fe
      LEFT JOIN logs_in_month lm
        ON lm.employee_id = fe.employee_id
    ),

    aggregated AS (
      SELECT
        el.employee_id,
        el.employee_code,
        el.full_name,

        -- 4a) Count distinct dates where the employee actually checked in
        COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL)
          AS cnt_present,

        -- 4b) Count “late” days: check_in_time > shift_start_time + 15 minutes
        COUNT(*) FILTER (
          WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL
            AND el.shift_start_time IS NOT NULL
            AND (el.check_in_time - el.shift_start_time) > INTERVAL '00:15:00'
        ) AS total_lates,

        -- 4c) Build “total_on_days” as TEXT, appending “(with rules)” or “(without rules)”
        CASE
          WHEN el.employment_rule = 'company_management' THEN
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            ) || ' (with rules)'
          WHEN el.employment_rule = 'self_management' THEN
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            ) || ' (without rules)'
          ELSE
            -- If employment_rule is NULL or something else, just show number
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            )
        END AS total_on_days,

        -- 4d) Compute “score_out_of_5” = ((present_days – total_lates)/workdays) × 5, rounded to one decimal
        CASE
          WHEN _total_weekdays = 0 THEN 0.0
          ELSE
            ROUND(
              (
                ( COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL)
                  -
                  COUNT(*) FILTER (
                    WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL
                      AND el.shift_start_time IS NOT NULL
                      AND (el.check_in_time - el.shift_start_time) > INTERVAL '00:15:00'
                  )
                )::NUMERIC
                / _total_weekdays::NUMERIC
              ) * 5.0
            , 1 )
        END AS score_out_of_5,

        -- 4e) “From Date” and “To Date” as text “Month / YYYY”
        TO_CHAR(_start_date, 'TMMonth / YYYY') AS from_month_year,
        TO_CHAR(_end_date,   'TMMonth / YYYY') AS to_month_year,

        -- 4f) Window function to compute total row count across all employees (for pagination)
        COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
      FROM emp_logs el
      GROUP BY
        el.employee_id,
        el.employee_code,
        el.full_name,
        el.shift_start_time,
        el.shift_end_time,
        el.employment_rule
    )

    SELECT
      a.employee_id,
      a.employee_code,
      a.full_name,
      a.total_on_days,
      a.total_lates,
      a.score_out_of_5,
      a.from_month_year,
      a.to_month_year,
      a.overall_count
    FROM aggregated a
    ORDER BY a.full_name
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_punctuality_report_month(p_year integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_month integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_name_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, total_on_days text, total_lates bigint, score_out_of_5 numeric, from_month_year text, to_month_year text, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE
AS $function$
DECLARE
  _year_int       INTEGER;
  _month_int      INTEGER;
  _start_date     DATE;
  _end_date       DATE;
  _total_weekdays INTEGER;
BEGIN
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 1) Determine “target year/month”:
  --    If p_year/p_month are NULL, default to the previous calendar month.
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  IF p_year IS NULL OR p_month IS NULL THEN
    -- Get the first day of the *current* month:
    _year_int := EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT;
    _month_int := EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT;
    -- Roll back one month:
    IF _month_int = 1 THEN
      _year_int := _year_int - 1;
      _month_int := 12;
    ELSE
      _month_int := _month_int - 1;
    END IF;
  ELSE
    _year_int  := p_year;
    _month_int := p_month;
  END IF;

  -- 2) Compute the first‐day and last‐day of that target month:
  _start_date := MAKE_DATE(_year_int, _month_int, 1);
  _end_date   := (_start_date + INTERVAL '1 MONTH')::DATE - 1;

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 3) Count how many “workdays” (Mon→Fri) exist in that month
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  SELECT COUNT(*)
    INTO _total_weekdays
    FROM GENERATE_SERIES(_start_date, _end_date, '1 day') AS g(day_dt)
   WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM g.day_dt) < 6;  -- ISODOW: 1=Mon .. 5=Fri

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 4) Build the filtered-employee set, join logs_in_month, then aggregate
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  RETURN QUERY
    WITH filtered_emps AS (
      SELECT
        e.id               AS employee_id,
        e.employee_code    AS employee_code,
        c.first_name,
        c.second_name,
        c.third_name,
        c.forth_name,
        CONCAT_WS(
          ' ',
          c.first_name,
          c.second_name,
          c.third_name,
          c.forth_name
        )                   AS full_name,
        e.shift_start_time AS shift_start_time,
        e.shift_end_time   AS shift_end_time,
        et.rules           AS employment_rule
      FROM public.employees e
      JOIN public.candidates c
        ON c.id = e.candidate_id
      LEFT JOIN public.employment_types et
        ON et.id = e.employment_type_id
      WHERE
        -- (a) If p_employee_id is supplied, only that employee; otherwise all
        ( p_employee_id IS NULL OR e.id = p_employee_id )
        AND
        -- (b) If name search is supplied, filter by concatenated full name
        ( p_name_search IS NULL
          OR CONCAT_WS(
               ' ',
               c.first_name,
               c.second_name,
               c.third_name,
               c.forth_name
             ) ILIKE '%' || p_name_search || '%' )
    ),

    logs_in_month AS (
      SELECT
        dl.employee_id,
        dl.date           AS log_date,
        dl.check_in_time,
        dl.check_out_time
      FROM public.daily_attendance_logs dl
      WHERE dl.date BETWEEN _start_date AND _end_date
        AND EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM dl.date) < 6  -- only Mon→Fri
    ),

    emp_logs AS (
      SELECT
        fe.employee_id,
        fe.employee_code,
        fe.full_name,
        fe.shift_start_time,
        fe.shift_end_time,
        fe.employment_rule,
        lm.log_date,
        lm.check_in_time,
        lm.check_out_time
      FROM filtered_emps fe
      LEFT JOIN logs_in_month lm
        ON lm.employee_id = fe.employee_id
    ),

    aggregated AS (
      SELECT
        el.employee_id,
        el.employee_code,
        el.full_name,

        -- 4a) Count distinct dates where the employee actually checked in
        COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL)
          AS cnt_present,

        -- 4b) Count “late” days:
        --     check_in_time > shift_start_time + 15 minutes
        COUNT(*) FILTER (
          WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL
            AND el.shift_start_time IS NOT NULL
            AND (el.check_in_time - el.shift_start_time) > INTERVAL '00:15:00'
        ) AS total_lates,

        -- 4c) Build “total_on_days” as TEXT, appending “(with rules)” or “(without rules)”
        CASE
          WHEN el.employment_rule = 'company_management' THEN
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            ) || ' (with rules)'
          WHEN el.employment_rule = 'self_management' THEN
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            ) || ' (without rules)'
          ELSE
            -- If employment_rule is NULL or something else, just show the number
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            )
        END AS total_on_days,

        -- 4d) Compute “score_out_of_5”:
        --      ((present_days – total_lates) / workdays) × 5, rounded to one decimal
        CASE
          WHEN _total_weekdays = 0 THEN 0.0
          ELSE
            ROUND(
              (
                ( COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL)
                  -
                  COUNT(*) FILTER (
                    WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL
                      AND el.shift_start_time IS NOT NULL
                      AND (el.check_in_time - el.shift_start_time) > INTERVAL '00:15:00'
                  )
                )::NUMERIC
                / _total_weekdays::NUMERIC
              ) * 5.0
            , 1 )
        END AS score_out_of_5,

        -- 4e) “From Date” and “To Date” as text “Month / YYYY”
        TO_CHAR(_start_date, 'TMMonth / YYYY') AS from_month_year,
        TO_CHAR(_end_date,   'TMMonth / YYYY') AS to_month_year,

        -- 4f) Window function for total row count (for pagination)
        COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
      FROM emp_logs el
      GROUP BY
        el.employee_id,
        el.employee_code,
        el.full_name,
        el.shift_start_time,
        el.shift_end_time,
        el.employment_rule
    )

    SELECT
      a.employee_id,
      a.employee_code,
      a.full_name,
      a.total_on_days,
      a.total_lates,
      a.score_out_of_5,
      a.from_month_year,
      a.to_month_year,
      a.overall_count
    FROM aggregated a
    ORDER BY a.full_name
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_date_wise_attendance_transactions(p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_code_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_name_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, designation_name text, check_in_time time without time zone, check_out_time time without time zone, break_start_time time without time zone, break_end_time time without time zone, break_availed interval, time_in_office interval, work_time interval, late_time interval, overtime interval, informed boolean, informed_early_exit boolean, overtime_approved boolean, attendance_date date, created_at timestamp with time zone, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE
AS $function$
BEGIN
  -------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 1) We start from daily_attendance_logs (inner‐joined to employees),
  --    because we only want rows that actually exist in the logs table.
  -- 2) We filter by employee_id, code substring, or name substring if provided.
  -- 3) We pull in each log’s stored intervals (break_availed, time_in_office)
  -- 4) We compute work_time = time_in_office – break_availed
  -- 5) We compute late_time & overtime from shift_start/end
  -- 6) We order by created_at DESC (newest log first)
  -- 7) We window‐count all matching rows into overall_count
  -------------------------------------------------------------------
  RETURN QUERY
    WITH 
    -- A) “filtered_emps” collects employees matching ID / code / name filters
    filtered_emps AS (
      SELECT
        e.id AS employee_id,
        e.employee_code,
        d.name         AS designation_name,
        CONCAT_WS(
          ' ',
          c.first_name,
          c.second_name,
          c.third_name,
          c.forth_name
        )               AS full_name,
        e.shift_start_time,
        e.shift_end_time
      FROM public.employees e
      JOIN public.candidates c
        ON c.id = e.candidate_id
      LEFT JOIN public.designations d
        ON d.id = e.designation_type
      WHERE
        ( p_employee_id IS NULL OR e.id = p_employee_id )
        AND
        ( p_code_search IS NULL 
          OR e.employee_code ILIKE '%' || p_code_search || '%' )
        AND
        ( p_name_search IS NULL 
          OR CONCAT_WS(
               ' ',
               c.first_name,
               c.second_name,
               c.third_name,
               c.forth_name
             ) ILIKE '%' || p_name_search || '%' )
    ),

    -- B) “joined_logs” pairs each matching employee with each of their log rows
    joined_logs AS (
      SELECT
        fe.employee_id,
        fe.employee_code,
        fe.full_name,
        fe.designation_name,
        fe.shift_start_time,
        fe.shift_end_time,
        dl.date            AS attendance_date,
        dl.created_at      AS created_at,
        dl.check_in_time,
        dl.check_out_time,
        dl.break_start_time,
        dl.break_end_time,
        dl.break_availed,
        dl.time_in_office,
        dl.informed,
        dl.informed_early_exit,
        dl.overtime_approved
      FROM filtered_emps fe
      JOIN public.daily_attendance_logs dl
        ON dl.employee_id = fe.employee_id
    ),

    -- C) “computed” does the interval math and “late”/“overtime” calc
    computed AS (
      SELECT
        jl.employee_id,
        jl.employee_code,
        jl.full_name,
        jl.designation_name,
        jl.attendance_date,
        jl.check_in_time,
        jl.check_out_time,
        jl.break_start_time,
        jl.break_end_time,
        -- already stored in table:
        jl.break_availed,
        jl.time_in_office,
        -- compute work_time:
        CASE
          WHEN jl.time_in_office IS NULL 
            OR jl.break_availed IS NULL 
            THEN NULL
          ELSE (jl.time_in_office - jl.break_availed)::interval
        END AS work_time,
        -- compute late_time = GREATEST(check_in_time - shift_start_time, '00:00:00')
        CASE
          WHEN jl.check_in_time    IS NULL 
            OR jl.shift_start_time IS NULL 
            THEN NULL
          ELSE GREATEST(
                 (jl.check_in_time - jl.shift_start_time),
                 '00:00:00'
               )::interval
        END AS late_time,
        -- compute overtime = GREATEST(check_out_time - shift_end_time, '00:00:00')
        CASE
          WHEN jl.check_out_time   IS NULL 
            OR jl.shift_end_time   IS NULL 
            THEN NULL
          ELSE GREATEST(
                 (jl.check_out_time - jl.shift_end_time),
                 '00:00:00'
               )::interval
        END AS overtime,
        jl.informed,
        jl.informed_early_exit,
        jl.overtime_approved,
        jl.created_at
      FROM joined_logs jl
    ),

    -- D) Number & paginate, while also capturing total matching row count
    numbered AS (
      SELECT
        c.*,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS overall_count
      FROM computed c
      ORDER BY c.created_at DESC, c.employee_code
      LIMIT p_limit
      OFFSET p_offset
    )

    SELECT
      n.employee_id,
      n.employee_code,
      n.full_name,
      n.designation_name,
      n.check_in_time,
      n.check_out_time,
      n.break_start_time,
      n.break_end_time,
      n.break_availed,
      n.time_in_office,
      n.work_time,
      n.late_time,
      n.overtime,
      n.informed,
      n.informed_early_exit,
      n.overtime_approved,
      n.attendance_date,
      n.created_at,
      n.overall_count
    FROM numbered n;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_monthly_attendance_report(p_year integer, p_month integer, p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_code_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, shift_start_time time without time zone, shift_end_time time without time zone, total_time_in_office interval, total_break_availed interval, total_overtime interval, total_dates bigint, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
DECLARE
    _counts RECORD;
BEGIN
    /* … count step remains the same … */

    SELECT
      COUNT(DISTINCT l.employee_id) AS cnt
    INTO _counts
    FROM daily_attendance_logs AS l
    JOIN employees AS e ON e.id = l.employee_id
    WHERE
        date_part('year', l.date)  = p_year
      AND date_part('month', l.date) = p_month
      AND (p_employee_id IS NULL OR l.employee_id = p_employee_id)
      AND (
           p_code_search IS NULL
        OR e.employee_code ILIKE ('%' || p_code_search || '%')
      );

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
      e.id                                    AS employee_id,
      e.employee_code                         AS employee_code,
      COALESCE(
        c.first_name || ' ' || c.second_name || ' ' || c.third_name,
        ''                                     )           AS full_name,
      e.shift_start_time                      AS shift_start_time,
      e.shift_end_time                        AS shift_end_time,
      COALESCE( SUM(l.time_in_office), '00:00:00'::interval )       AS total_time_in_office,
      COALESCE( SUM(l.break_availed),    '00:00:00'::interval )     AS total_break_availed,
      COALESCE( SUM(l.overtime),         '00:00:00'::interval )     AS total_overtime,
      COUNT(DISTINCT l.date)               AS total_dates,          -- ← now matches bigint
      _counts.cnt                           AS overall_count
    FROM
      daily_attendance_logs AS l
      JOIN employees AS e ON e.id = l.employee_id
      LEFT JOIN candidates AS c ON c.id = e.candidate_id
    WHERE
        date_part('year', l.date)  = p_year
      AND date_part('month', l.date) = p_month
      AND (p_employee_id IS NULL OR l.employee_id = p_employee_id)
      AND (
           p_code_search IS NULL
        OR e.employee_code ILIKE ('%' || p_code_search || '%')
      )
    GROUP BY
      e.id,
      e.employee_code,
      c.first_name, c.second_name, c.third_name,
      e.shift_start_time, e.shift_end_time,
      _counts.cnt
    ORDER BY
      e.employee_code
    OFFSET p_offset
    LIMIT  p_limit;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_punctuality_report_monthly(p_year integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_month integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_name_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, total_on_days text, total_lates bigint, score_out_of_5 numeric, from_month_year text, to_month_year text, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 STABLE
AS $function$
DECLARE
  _year_int       INTEGER;
  _month_int      INTEGER;
  _start_date     DATE;
  _end_date       DATE;
  _total_weekdays INTEGER;
BEGIN
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 1) Determine “target year/month”:
  --    If p_year/p_month are NULL, default to the previous calendar month.
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  IF p_year IS NULL OR p_month IS NULL THEN
    -- Get the first day of the *current* month:
    _year_int := EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT;
    _month_int := EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT;
    -- Roll back one month:
    IF _month_int = 1 THEN
      _year_int := _year_int - 1;
      _month_int := 12;
    ELSE
      _month_int := _month_int - 1;
    END IF;
  ELSE
    _year_int  := p_year;
    _month_int := p_month;
  END IF;

  -- 2) Compute the first‐day and last‐day of that target month:
  _start_date := MAKE_DATE(_year_int, _month_int, 1);
  _end_date   := (_start_date + INTERVAL '1 MONTH')::DATE - 1;

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 3) Count how many “workdays” (Mon→Fri) exist in that month
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  SELECT COUNT(*)
    INTO _total_weekdays
    FROM GENERATE_SERIES(_start_date, _end_date, '1 day') AS g(day_dt)
   WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM g.day_dt) < 6;  -- ISODOW: 1=Mon .. 5=Fri

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 4) Build the filtered-employee set, join logs_in_month, then aggregate
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  RETURN QUERY
    WITH filtered_emps AS (
      SELECT
        e.id               AS employee_id,
        e.employee_code    AS employee_code,
        c.first_name,
        c.second_name,
        c.third_name,
        c.forth_name,
        CONCAT_WS(
          ' ',
          c.first_name,
          c.second_name,
          c.third_name,
          c.forth_name
        )                   AS full_name,
        e.shift_start_time AS shift_start_time,
        e.shift_end_time   AS shift_end_time,
        et.rules           AS employment_rule
      FROM public.employees e
      JOIN public.candidates c
        ON c.id = e.candidate_id
      LEFT JOIN public.employment_types et
        ON et.id = e.employment_type_id
      WHERE
        -- (a) If p_employee_id is supplied, only that employee; otherwise all
        ( p_employee_id IS NULL OR e.id = p_employee_id )
        AND
        -- (b) If name search is supplied, filter by concatenated full name
        ( p_name_search IS NULL
          OR CONCAT_WS(
               ' ',
               c.first_name,
               c.second_name,
               c.third_name,
               c.forth_name
             ) ILIKE '%' || p_name_search || '%' )
    ),

    logs_in_month AS (
      SELECT
        dl.employee_id,
        dl.date           AS log_date,
        dl.check_in_time,
        dl.check_out_time
      FROM public.daily_attendance_logs dl
      WHERE dl.date BETWEEN _start_date AND _end_date
        AND EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM dl.date) < 6  -- only Mon→Fri
    ),

    emp_logs AS (
      SELECT
        fe.employee_id,
        fe.employee_code,
        fe.full_name,
        fe.shift_start_time,
        fe.shift_end_time,
        fe.employment_rule,
        lm.log_date,
        lm.check_in_time,
        lm.check_out_time
      FROM filtered_emps fe
      LEFT JOIN logs_in_month lm
        ON lm.employee_id = fe.employee_id
    ),

    aggregated AS (
      SELECT
        el.employee_id,
        el.employee_code,
        el.full_name,

        -- 4a) Count distinct dates where the employee actually checked in
        COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL)
          AS cnt_present,

        -- 4b) Count “late” days:
        --     check_in_time > shift_start_time + 15 minutes
        COUNT(*) FILTER (
          WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL
            AND el.shift_start_time IS NOT NULL
            AND (el.check_in_time - el.shift_start_time) > INTERVAL '00:15:00'
        ) AS total_lates,

        -- 4c) Build “total_on_days” as TEXT, appending “(with rules)” or “(without rules)”
        CASE
          WHEN el.employment_rule = 'company_management' THEN
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            ) || ' (with rules)'
          WHEN el.employment_rule = 'self_management' THEN
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            ) || ' (without rules)'
          ELSE
            -- If employment_rule is NULL or something else, just show the number
            TO_CHAR(
              COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL),
              'FM9990'
            )
        END AS total_on_days,

        -- 4d) Compute “score_out_of_5”:
        --      ((present_days – total_lates) / workdays) × 5, rounded to one decimal
        CASE
          WHEN _total_weekdays = 0 THEN 0.0
          ELSE
            ROUND(
              (
                ( COUNT(DISTINCT el.log_date) FILTER (WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL)
                  -
                  COUNT(*) FILTER (
                    WHERE el.check_in_time IS NOT NULL
                      AND el.shift_start_time IS NOT NULL
                      AND (el.check_in_time - el.shift_start_time) > INTERVAL '00:15:00'
                  )
                )::NUMERIC
                / _total_weekdays::NUMERIC
              ) * 5.0
            , 1 )
        END AS score_out_of_5,

        -- 4e) “From Date” and “To Date” as text “Month / YYYY”
        TO_CHAR(_start_date, 'TMMonth / YYYY') AS from_month_year,
        TO_CHAR(_end_date,   'TMMonth / YYYY') AS to_month_year,

        -- 4f) Window function for total row count (for pagination)
        COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
      FROM emp_logs el
      GROUP BY
        el.employee_id,
        el.employee_code,
        el.full_name,
        el.shift_start_time,
        el.shift_end_time,
        el.employment_rule
    )

    SELECT
      a.employee_id,
      a.employee_code,
      a.full_name,
      a.total_on_days,
      a.total_lates,
      a.score_out_of_5,
      a.from_month_year,
      a.to_month_year,
      a.overall_count
    FROM aggregated a
    ORDER BY a.full_name
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_weekly_attendance_register(p_week_start date DEFAULT NULL::date, p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_code_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_name_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, designation_name text, sun_col text, mon_col text, tue_col text, wed_col text, thu_col text, fri_col text, sat_col text, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
DECLARE
  _week_start_sunday DATE;
BEGIN
  -- 1) Compute “this week’s Sunday” if not provided:
  IF p_week_start IS NULL THEN
    _week_start_sunday := current_date - extract(dow FROM current_date)::INT;
  ELSE
    _week_start_sunday := p_week_start;
  END IF;

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 2) Build the CTE chain including optional p_employee_id filter:
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  RETURN QUERY
  WITH filtered_emps AS (
    SELECT
      e.id                AS employee_id,
      e.employee_code     AS employee_code,
      d.name              AS designation_name,
      CONCAT_WS(
        ' ',
        c.first_name,
        c.second_name,
        c.third_name,
        c.forth_name
      )                    AS full_name
    FROM employees e
    JOIN candidates c
      ON c.id = e.candidate_id
    LEFT JOIN designations d
      ON d.id = e.designation_type
    WHERE
      -- 2A) If p_employee_id IS NOT NULL, only that employee; otherwise include all
      (p_employee_id IS NULL OR e.id = p_employee_id)
      AND
      -- 2B) Filter by code substring if provided
      (p_code_search IS NULL OR e.employee_code ILIKE '%' || p_code_search || '%')
      AND
      -- 2C) Filter by name substring if provided
      (p_name_search IS NULL
       OR CONCAT_WS(' ',
            c.first_name,
            c.second_name,
            c.third_name,
            c.forth_name
          ) ILIKE '%' || p_name_search || '%')
  ),

  emp_week AS (
    SELECT
      fe.employee_id,
      fe.employee_code,
      fe.full_name,
      fe.designation_name,
      gs AS log_date
    FROM filtered_emps fe
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
      SELECT generate_series(
        _week_start_sunday,
        _week_start_sunday + 6,
        '1 day'::interval
      )::date AS gs
    ) AS daily
  ),

  logs_joined AS (
    SELECT
      ew.employee_id,
      ew.employee_code,
      ew.full_name,
      ew.designation_name,
      ew.log_date,
      dl.check_in_time,
      dl.check_out_time,
      dl.time_in_office,
      dl.late_time,
      dl.overtime
    FROM emp_week ew
    LEFT JOIN daily_attendance_logs dl
      ON dl.employee_id = ew.employee_id
     AND dl.date        = ew.log_date
  ),

  logs_with_display AS (
    SELECT
      lj.employee_id,
      lj.employee_code,
      lj.full_name,
      lj.designation_name,
      lj.log_date,
      CASE
        -- 2D) If the log_date is in the future, display “–”
        WHEN lj.log_date > current_date THEN '-'
        -- 2E) If no check_in_time and it’s today or past, display “OFF”
        WHEN lj.check_in_time IS NULL THEN 'OFF'
        ELSE
          'I:' || TO_CHAR(lj.check_in_time, 'HH24:MI')
          || '; O:' || TO_CHAR(lj.check_out_time, 'HH24:MI')
          || '; W:' || TO_CHAR(lj.time_in_office, 'HH24:MI:SS')
          || '; L:' || COALESCE(TO_CHAR(lj.late_time, 'HH24:MI:SS'), '00:00:00')
      END AS day_display
    FROM logs_joined lj
  ),

  pivoted AS (
    SELECT
      lwd.employee_id,
      lwd.employee_code,
      lwd.full_name,
      lwd.designation_name,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 0 THEN day_display END) AS sun_col,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 1 THEN day_display END) AS mon_col,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 2 THEN day_display END) AS tue_col,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 3 THEN day_display END) AS wed_col,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 4 THEN day_display END) AS thu_col,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 5 THEN day_display END) AS fri_col,
      MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(dow FROM log_date) = 6 THEN day_display END) AS sat_col
    FROM logs_with_display lwd
    GROUP BY
      lwd.employee_id,
      lwd.employee_code,
      lwd.full_name,
      lwd.designation_name
  ),

  counted AS (
    SELECT
      p.*,
      COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
    FROM pivoted p
    ORDER BY p.employee_code
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset
  )

  SELECT
    c.employee_id,
    c.employee_code,
    c.full_name,
    c.designation_name,
    c.sun_col,
    c.mon_col,
    c.tue_col,
    c.wed_col,
    c.thu_col,
    c.fri_col,
    c.sat_col,
    c.overall_count
  FROM counted c;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_daily_attendance_transactions(p_date date DEFAULT NULL::date, p_full_name text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_code_search text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_employee_id integer DEFAULT NULL::integer, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(employee_id integer, employee_code text, full_name text, check_in_time time without time zone, check_out_time time without time zone, time_in_office interval, break_availed interval, work_time interval, late_time interval, overtime interval, informed boolean, informed_early_exit boolean, overtime_approved boolean, overall_count bigint)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  WITH daily_logs AS (
    SELECT
      dl.employee_id,
      e.employee_code,
      CONCAT_WS(' ',
        c.first_name,
        c.second_name,
        c.third_name,
        c.forth_name
      ) AS full_name,
      dl.check_in_time,
      dl.check_out_time,
      dl.time_in_office,
      dl.break_availed,
      dl.work_time,
      dl.late_time,
      dl.overtime,
      dl.informed,
      dl.informed_early_exit,
      dl.overtime_approved
    FROM public.daily_attendance_logs dl
    JOIN public.employees e
      ON e.id = dl.employee_id
    JOIN public.candidates c
      ON c.id = e.candidate_id
    WHERE
      dl.date = p_date
      AND (p_code_search IS NULL OR e.employee_code ILIKE '%' || p_code_search || '%')
      AND (p_employee_id IS NULL OR dl.employee_id = p_employee_id)
        AND (
    p_full_name IS NULL
    OR CONCAT_WS(
         ' ',
         c.first_name,
         c.second_name,
         c.third_name,
         c.forth_name
      ) ILIKE '%' || p_full_name || '%'
  )
  ),
  counted AS (
    SELECT
      *,
      COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
    FROM daily_logs
    ORDER BY employee_code
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset
  )
  SELECT
    employee_id,
    employee_code,
    full_name,
    check_in_time,
    check_out_time,
    time_in_office,
    break_availed,
    work_time,
    late_time,
    overtime,
    informed,
    informed_early_exit,
    overtime_approved,
    overall_count
  FROM counted;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_my_announcements(
  p_employee_id     INTEGER,
  p_company_id      INTEGER    DEFAULT NULL,
  p_search          TEXT       DEFAULT NULL,
  p_limit           INT        DEFAULT 10,
  p_offset          INT        DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  announcement_id     INTEGER,
  status              TEXT,
  reference_no        TEXT,
  title               TEXT,
  active_date         DATE,
  expiry_date         DATE,
  announcement_status TEXT,
  created_by_id       INTEGER,
  created_by_name     TEXT,
  attachment_path     TEXT,
  is_mandatory        BOOLEAN,
  for_organization    BOOLEAN,
  read_status         TEXT,
  is_liked            BOOLEAN,
  overall_count       BIGINT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
AS $$
DECLARE
  _dept_id INTEGER;
BEGIN
  -- find this user�s department
  SELECT organizational_unit_id
    INTO _dept_id
    FROM public.employees
   WHERE id = p_employee_id;

  RETURN QUERY
    WITH filtered AS (
      SELECT
        a.id                   AS announcement_id,
        a.status,
        a.reference_no,
        a.title,
        a.active_date,
        a.expiry_date,
        CASE
          WHEN a.expiry_date IS NOT NULL
               AND a.expiry_date >= CURRENT_DATE
            THEN 'active'
          ELSE 'expired'
        END                   AS announcement_status,
        a.created_by_id,
        CONCAT_WS(' ',
          cb.first_name,
          cb.second_name,
          cb.third_name,
          cb.forth_name
        )                     AS created_by_name,
        a.attachment_path,
        a.is_mandatory,
        a.for_organization,
        -- determine read vs unread
        CASE
          WHEN EXISTS(
            SELECT 1
              FROM public.announcement_acknowledgements ack
             WHERE ack.announcement_id = a.id
               AND ack.employee_id     = p_employee_id
               AND ack.acknowledged    = TRUE
          ) THEN 'read'
          ELSE 'unread'
        END                   AS read_status,
        -- determine liked status
        EXISTS(
          SELECT 1
            FROM public.announcement_acknowledgements ack2
           WHERE ack2.announcement_id = a.id
             AND ack2.employee_id     = p_employee_id
             AND ack2.is_liked        = TRUE
        )                     AS is_liked
      FROM public.announcements a
      JOIN public.employees ce
        ON ce.id = a.created_by_id
      JOIN public.candidates cb
        ON cb.id = ce.candidate_id
      WHERE
        a.active_date <= CURRENT_DATE
        AND (p_company_id IS NULL OR a.company_id = p_company_id)
        AND (
          p_search IS NULL
          OR a.title        ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
          OR a.reference_no ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
        )
        AND (
          a.for_organization = TRUE
          OR (
            a.for_organization = FALSE
            AND EXISTS (
              SELECT 1
                FROM public.department_announcement da
               WHERE da.announcement_id = a.id
                 AND da.department_id   = _dept_id
            )
          )
        )
    ),
    numbered AS (
      SELECT
        f.*,
        COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
      FROM filtered f
      ORDER BY f.active_date DESC NULLS LAST
      LIMIT p_limit
      OFFSET p_offset
    )
  SELECT
    n.announcement_id,
    n.status,
    n.reference_no,
    n.title,
    n.active_date,
    n.expiry_date,
    n.announcement_status,
    n.created_by_id,
    n.created_by_name,
    n.attachment_path,
    n.is_mandatory,
    n.for_organization,
    n.read_status,
    n.is_liked,
    n.overall_count
  FROM numbered n;
END;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_my_announcements(
  p_employee_id     INTEGER,
  p_company_id      INTEGER    DEFAULT NULL,
  p_search          TEXT       DEFAULT NULL,
  p_limit           INT        DEFAULT 10,
  p_offset          INT        DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  announcement_id     INTEGER,
  status              TEXT,
  reference_no        TEXT,
  title               TEXT,

  created_by_name     TEXT,
  attachment_path     TEXT,
  is_mandatory        BOOLEAN,
  for_organization    BOOLEAN,
  read_status         TEXT,
  overall_count       BIGINT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
AS $$
DECLARE
  _dept_id INTEGER;
BEGIN
  -- find this user�s department
  SELECT organizational_unit_id
    INTO _dept_id
    FROM public.employees
   WHERE id = p_employee_id;

  RETURN QUERY
    WITH filtered AS (
      SELECT
        a.id                   AS announcement_id,
        a.status,
        a.reference_no,
        a.title,
        a.active_date,
        a.expiry_date,
        CASE
          WHEN a.expiry_date IS NOT NULL
               AND a.expiry_date >= CURRENT_DATE
            THEN 'active'
          ELSE 'expired'
        END                   AS announcement_status,
        a.created_by_id,
        CONCAT_WS(' ',
          cb.first_name,
          cb.second_name,
          cb.third_name,
          cb.forth_name
        )                     AS created_by_name,
        a.attachment_path,
        a.is_mandatory,
        a.for_organization,
        -- determine read vs unread
        CASE
          WHEN EXISTS(
            SELECT 1
              FROM public.announcement_acknowledgements ack
             WHERE ack.announcement_id = a.id
               AND ack.employee_id     = p_employee_id
               AND ack.acknowledged    = TRUE
          ) THEN 'read'
          ELSE 'unread'
        END                   AS read_status
      FROM public.announcements a
      JOIN public.employees ce
        ON ce.id = a.created_by_id
      JOIN public.candidates cb
        ON cb.id = ce.candidate_id
      WHERE
        a.active_date <= CURRENT_DATE
        AND (p_company_id IS NULL OR a.company_id = p_company_id)
        AND (
          p_search IS NULL
          OR a.title        ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
          OR a.reference_no ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
        )
        AND (
          a.for_organization = TRUE
          OR (
            a.for_organization = FALSE
            AND EXISTS (
              SELECT 1
                FROM public.department_announcement da
               WHERE da.announcement_id = a.id
                 AND da.department_id   = _dept_id
            )
          )
        )
    ),
    numbered AS (
      SELECT
        f.*,
        COUNT(*) OVER() AS overall_count
      FROM filtered f
      ORDER BY f.active_date DESC NULLS LAST
      LIMIT p_limit
      OFFSET p_offset
    )
  SELECT
    n.announcement_id,
    n.status,
    n.reference_no,
    n.title,
    n.active_date,
    n.expiry_date,
    n.announcement_status,
    n.created_by_id,
    n.created_by_name,
    n.attachment_path,
    n.is_mandatory,
    n.for_organization,
    n.read_status,
    n.overall_count
  FROM numbered n;
END;
$$;

-- CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_my_mandatory_announcements(
--   p_employee_id   INTEGER,
--   p_company_id    INTEGER    DEFAULT NULL
-- )
-- RETURNS TABLE (
--   announcement_id     INTEGER,
--   status              TEXT,
--   reference_no        TEXT,
--   title               TEXT,
--   description         TEXT,
--   active_date         DATE,
--   expiry_date         DATE,

--   attachment_path     TEXT,
--   is_mandatory        BOOLEAN,
--   for_organization    BOOLEAN,
--   read_status         TEXT
-- )
-- LANGUAGE plpgsql
-- STABLE
-- AS $$

--   SELECT organizational_unit_id
--     INTO _dept_id
--     FROM public.employees
--    WHERE id = p_employee_id;

--   RETURN QUERY
--     SELECT
--       a.id                   AS announcement_id,
--       a.status,
--       a.reference_no,
--       a.title,

--       a.active_date,

--       CASE

--       CONCAT_WS(' ',
--         cb.first_name,

--         cb.forth_name
--       )                       AS created_by_name,

--       CASE
--         WHEN EXISTS (

--             FROM public.announcement_acknowledgements ack
--            WHERE ack.announcement_id = a.id
--              AND ack.employee_id     = p_employee_id
--              AND ack.acknowledged    = TRUE
--         ) THEN 'read'
--         ELSE 'unread'
--       END                     AS read_status
--     FROM public.announcements a
--     JOIN public.employees ce
--       ON ce.id = a.created_by_id
--     JOIN public.candidates cb
--       ON cb.id = ce.candidate_id
--     WHERE

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_announcement_summary(
    p_created_by_id integer
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    status TEXT,
    non_mandatory_count INTEGER,
    mandatory_count INTEGER
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY

  -- ACTIVE: today ? [active_date, expiry_date]
  SELECT
    'active'::TEXT,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_mandatory = FALSE),
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_mandatory = TRUE)
  FROM public.announcements
  WHERE (p_created_by_id IS NULL OR created_by_id = p_created_by_id)
    AND active_date <= CURRENT_DATE
    AND (expiry_date IS NULL OR expiry_date >= CURRENT_DATE)

  UNION ALL

  -- UPCOMING: active_date > today
  SELECT
    'upcoming'::TEXT,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_mandatory = FALSE),
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_mandatory = TRUE)
  FROM public.announcements
  WHERE (p_created_by_id IS NULL OR created_by_id = p_created_by_id)
    AND active_date > CURRENT_DATE

  UNION ALL

  -- EXPIRED: expiry_date < today
  SELECT
    'expired'::TEXT,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_mandatory = FALSE),
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_mandatory = TRUE)
  FROM public.announcements
  WHERE (p_created_by_id IS NULL OR created_by_id = p_created_by_id)
    AND expiry_date < CURRENT_DATE;
END;
$$;
-- Return events visible to this employee
JOIN public.employees ce ON ce.id = e.created_by_id
JOIN public.candidates cb ON cb.id = ce.candidate_id
AND (p_company_id IS NULL OR e.company_id = p_company_id)
WHEN b.end_date IS NOT NULL AND b.end_date::date >= _today THEN 'active'
OR (_branch_id IS NOT NULL AND b.branch_id = _branch_id)  -- if branch_id exists
WHERE ee.event_id = b.id AND ee.employee_id = p_employee_id
ORDER BY f.start_date::date DESC NULLS LAST
-- Return events visible to this employee
JOIN public.employees ce ON ce.id = e.created_by_id
JOIN public.candidates cb ON cb.id = ce.candidate_id
AND (p_company_id IS NULL OR e.company_id = p_company_id)
OR (_branch_id IS NOT NULL AND b.branch_id = _branch_id)  -- if branch_id exists
WHERE ee.event_id = b.id AND ee.employee_id = p_employee_id
ORDER BY f.start_date::date DESC NULLS LAST

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_unscheduled_candidates()
  RETURNS SETOF public.candidates
  LANGUAGE SQL
  STABLE
AS $$
  SELECT c.*
    FROM public.candidates c
   WHERE NOT EXISTS (
     SELECT 1
       FROM public.scheduled_interviews si
      WHERE si.candidate_id = c.id
   );
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_my_mandatory_announcements(
  p_employee_id INTEGER,
  p_company_id INTEGER
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  announcement_id INTEGER,
  status TEXT,
  reference_no TEXT,
  title TEXT,
  description TEXT,
  active_date DATE,
  expiry_date DATE,
  announcement_status TEXT,
  created_by_id INTEGER,
  created_by_name TEXT,
  attachment_path TEXT,
  is_mandatory BOOLEAN,
  for_organization BOOLEAN,
  read_status TEXT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
DECLARE
  _dept_id INTEGER;
BEGIN
  -- 1) Lookup this employee's department
  SELECT organizational_unit_id
    INTO _dept_id
    FROM public.employees
   WHERE id = p_employee_id;

  -- 2) Return all passed?activation, mandatory announcements NOT yet acknowledged
  RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
      a.id AS announcement_id,
      a.status,
      a.reference_no,
      a.title,
      a.description,
      a.active_date,
      a.expiry_date,
      CASE
        WHEN a.expiry_date IS NOT NULL AND a.expiry_date >= CURRENT_DATE
        THEN 'active'
        ELSE 'expired'
      END AS announcement_status,
      a.created_by_id,
      CONCAT_WS(' ', cb.first_name, cb.second_name, cb.third_name, cb.forth_name) AS created_by_name,
      a.attachment_path,
      a.is_mandatory,
      a.for_organization,
      CASE
        WHEN EXISTS (
          SELECT 1
          FROM public.announcement_acknowledgements ack
          WHERE ack.announcement_id = a.id
            AND ack.employee_id = p_employee_id
            AND ack.acknowledged = TRUE
        ) THEN 'read'
        ELSE 'unread'
      END AS read_status
    FROM public.announcements a
    JOIN public.employees ce ON ce.id = a.created_by_id
    JOIN public.candidates cb ON cb.id = ce.candidate_id
    WHERE
      a.active_date <= CURRENT_DATE
      AND a.is_mandatory = TRUE
      AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM public.announcement_acknowledgements ack
        WHERE ack.announcement_id = a.id
          AND ack.employee_id = p_employee_id
      )
      AND (p_company_id IS NULL OR a.company_id = p_company_id)
      AND (
        a.for_organization = TRUE
        OR (
          a.for_organization = FALSE
          AND EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM public.department_announcement da
            WHERE da.announcement_id = a.id
              AND da.department_id = _dept_id
          )
        )
      )
    ORDER BY a.active_date DESC NULLS LAST;
END;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_offboarding_requests(
  p_employment_type_ids integer[],
  p_status text,
  p_search text,
  p_offset integer,
  p_limit integer
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  id integer,
  termination_date date,
  reason text,
  status text,
  created_by integer,
  created_at timestamp with time zone,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone,
  employee jsonb
)
LANGUAGE sql
AS $$
  SELECT
    r.id,
    r.termination_date,
    r.reason,
    r.status,
    r.created_by,
    r.created_at,
    r.updated_at,
    jsonb_build_object(
      'id',    e.id,
      'code',  e.employee_code,
      'unitId', e.organizational_unit_id,
      'candidate', jsonb_build_object(
         'id',         c.id,
         'first_name', c.first_name,
         'family_name',c.family_name
      )
    ) AS employee
  FROM public.offboarding_requests r
  JOIN public.employees e ON e.id = r.employee_id
  JOIN public.candidates c ON c.id = e.candidate_id
  WHERE r.is_deleted = FALSE
    AND e.employment_type_id = ANY(p_employment_type_ids)
    AND (
      p_status IS NULL
      OR r.status = p_status
    )
    AND (
      p_search IS NULL
      OR (
        r.reason ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
        OR c.first_name ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
        OR c.family_name ILIKE '%' || p_search || '%'
      )
    )
  ORDER BY r.created_at DESC
  OFFSET p_offset
  LIMIT  p_limit;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_offboarding_requests_filtered_by_first_name(
  p_first_name TEXT,
  p_status TEXT,
  p_employment_type_ids INTEGER[],
  p_from INTEGER,
  p_to INTEGER,
  p_created_by INTEGER
)
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
DECLARE
  result JSONB;
BEGIN
  SELECT jsonb_agg(t)
  INTO result
  FROM (
    SELECT
      r.*,
      jsonb_build_object(
        'employee', jsonb_build_object(
          'details', to_jsonb(e),
          'candidate', to_jsonb(c)
        )
      ) AS employee_info
    FROM public.offboarding_requests r
    INNER JOIN public.employees e ON e.id = r.employee_id
    INNER JOIN public.candidates c ON c.id = e.candidate_id
    WHERE r.is_deleted = FALSE
      AND (p_status IS NULL OR r.status = p_status)
      AND (p_employment_type_ids IS NULL OR e.employment_type_id = ANY(p_employment_type_ids))
      AND (p_first_name IS NULL OR c.first_name ILIKE '%' || p_first_name || '%')
      AND (p_created_by IS NULL OR r.created_by = p_created_by)
    ORDER BY r.created_at DESC
    OFFSET p_from
    LIMIT (p_to - p_from + 1)
  ) t;

  RETURN COALESCE(result, '[]'::jsonb);
END;
$function$;
